title: telephium imperati subsp. imperati
intro: Telephium imperati subsp. imperati, also known as Imperata ceylanica or Khas grass, is a perennial grass native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Telephium imperati subsp. imperati include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and made into capsules or tinctures for oral consumption.
